hero.eyebrow: About Oneirox · AI Dream Interpretation · Dream Decoder
hero.title: The First Scientific AI Dream Interpretation Engine. Built Because Nothing Else Was.
hero.lead: Most dream tools give you a symbol table. Snake = betrayal. Water = emotion. Falling = loss of control.
hero.lead_strong: Oneirox answers the question those tables never could: not what your dream means in general — what your brain was specifically doing while it produced that image, on that night, in your life.

what_is.title: An AI Dream Decoder Grounded in Sleep Neuroscience
paragraphs: Oneirox is a free AI-powered dream interpretation tool — a dream decoder that reads what your brain was actually doing during sleep, not what ancient symbol tables say your dream should mean.

The Decode engine was built on a specific insight: every dream is a neurobiological event. The images your sleeping brain generates are precise, physiologically determined outputs of systems that were running while you slept.
The question isn't what a dream means. The question is what the brain was processing. Oneirox answers the second question, which automatically answers the first.

other.label: Every Other Dream Site
other.text: You type "snake." You get five possible meanings depending on tradition. You leave with no way to know which one is yours.

oneirox.label: Oneirox Dream Decoder
oneirox.text: You describe your dream. The AI identifies which neurobiological mechanism was active and explains what it means for your specific situation. One precise reading.

decode_output.title: Describe your dream. Get the neurobiological truth.
decode_output.subtitle: Three structured sections. Each one precise, grounded in peer-reviewed sleep research, written for the person who woke at 3am with a question — not a reader of textbooks.
bullets: The core diagnostic truth — what your brain was actually doing, in one sentence
The active mechanism: threat simulation, emotional tagging, somatic signal, or memory consolidation
The question this dream was trying to deliver to your waking mind

why_no_analogs.title: Every Dream Tool Gets This Wrong
why_no_analogs.intro: It took five years and fifty books to understand why every existing dream interpretation product produces the same result: symbol tables dressed in new technology. The problem isn't the technology. It's the premise.

points.title: They start with the symbol, not the brain
points.text: Dream dictionaries predate REM research by a century. Most AI tools digitize those tables at scale. Oneirox was built from the neuroscience up.

points.title: They ignore the body entirely
points.text: The chest pressure on waking. The cold that didn't lift. These are the most diagnostically accurate data points — and every existing tool discards them.

points.title: They give you five possible meanings
points.text: When a tool gives you every possible interpretation, it has given you nothing. Oneirox commits to one neurobiological reading based on what actually happened in your brain.

points.title: They never ask what your body felt
points.text: The Sensory Dream Mapper was built for exactly this gap. Nine neurobiological somatic profiles. The body knows first — Damasio established this.

author_section.title: The Science Behind Every Interpretation
author_section.intro: My name is Vigen. I spent more than five years at the intersection of sleep neuroscience, clinical attachment theory, and Jungian depth psychology — not as an academic, but as someone who found it necessary to understand what actually happens in dreams.
author_section.intro_strong: Not what they "mean" in the abstract. What the brain was specifically doing when it produced a specific image on a specific night of a specific life.
author_section.closing: I read not to build a citation library. I read because I wanted to know why this specific image, on this specific night, in this specific person's life — and why existing tools kept giving symbol tables instead of answers.

researchers.insight: What REM sleep does to emotional memory
Somatic markers — the body's own records of what matters
Two fear pathways — the body moves before the cortex
Social threat is physiologically identical to physical danger
Images explain physiological states already running
Stress, cortisol, and the hippocampus under load
The body's record of what the mind didn't fully process
Patterns that precede the individual mind

mechanisms.label: MECHANISM 01
mechanisms.title: Threat Simulation — Revonsuo
mechanisms.text: The amygdala generates rehearsal scenarios for unresolved threats — not as metaphor but as preparation.

mechanisms.label: MECHANISM 02
mechanisms.title: Emotional Memory Consolidation — Walker, Cartwright
mechanisms.text: REM sleep processes unresolved emotional charge. Recurring dreams about the same person or situation are frequently this process, looping because it hasn't completed.

mechanisms.label: MECHANISM 03
mechanisms.title: Somatic Signal — Damasio
mechanisms.text: Physical sensations on waking are often the most diagnostically accurate data. The Sensory Dream Mapper was built to read this layer before the narrative dissolves.

instruments.decoder: Describe your dream, receive a neurobiological analysis. The core diagnostic truth, the active mechanism, and the question your brain was trying to deliver.
instruments.mapper: Nine neurobiological somatic profiles. Map thermal state, tactile signatures, and body zones in 90 seconds. Send somatic context directly to Decode.

footer_cta.title: The signal was there when you woke up. Decode it now.
